results.rationale: When len > 3, enc is heap-allocated via zmalloc at line 1169 and is only released at line 1254 (`if (enc != tmp) lp_free(enc)`) on the normal return. The early return at line 1221 (lp_realloc failure) returns NULL without freeing enc, leaking it. The same gap exists at the line 1211 overflow return. Additionally line 1221 uses the realloc anti-pattern `lp = lp_realloc(lp,...)`: on failure lp becomes NULL and the caller loses the reference to the original listpack buffer. Both paths are feasible since lp_realloc is explicitly NULL-checked.
results.fix_suggestion: Free the heap-allocated enc before the early returns at lines 1211 and 1221 (e.g. `if (enc != tmp) lp_free(enc);` prior to `return NULL;`), and use a temporary pointer for lp_realloc so the original lp is not lost on failure.

results.rationale: When len > 3, 'enc' is heap-allocated via zmalloc at line 1169. It is only freed at line 1254 (if enc != tmp) on the normal return path. The early returns at line 1211 (new_listpack_bytes > UINT32_MAX) and line 1221 (lp_realloc failure) skip this cleanup, leaking the 'enc' buffer. Both early-return paths are feasible for len > 3, so allocated memory is not released on those paths.
results.fix_suggestion: Free the heap-allocated 'enc' before each early return: add 'if (enc != tmp) lp_free(enc);' before 'return NULL;' at lines 1211 and 1221.

results.rationale: lp is allocated via lpNew(0) at line 2841 just before a loop that parses zipmap entries. In this rdb.c conversion routine the loop body contains corruption/OOM error paths that release dupSearchDict and the original object then 'return NULL', but the freshly allocated listpack 'lp' is not freed on those early-return paths, so it leaks. lp is only ever assigned to o->ptr after the loop completes successfully.
results.fix_suggestion: Add lpFree(lp) (and lpSafeToAdd/error cleanup) on every error/return path inside the zipmap-to-listpack conversion loop before returning NULL.
